title: Teacher Quality and Student Outcomes: Evidence from Value-Added Models and Long-Run Data

sections.title: Interaction of Class Size and Teacher VA
sections.label: sec:class-va-interact

qa.question: How many student-year observations are in the sample?
What is the standard deviation of teacher value-added in math?
qa.answer: 0.14 SD units

qa.question: What is the mean class size?
What fraction of students are eligible for free/reduced lunch?
qa.answer: 0.48 (48 percent)

tables.caption: Teacher Value-Added Estimation: Forecast Bias Tests
tables.label: tab:va-estimation

qa.question: Is teacher VA a forecast-unbiased predictor of student test scores?
qa.answer: Yes, the coefficient is approximately 1.0 in all specifications and we cannot reject beta=1

qa.question: What controls are included in column 4?
qa.answer: Student demographics, lagged test scores (cubic), and school fixed effects

qa.question: What is the p-value for the test that beta=1 in column 3?
Does a high-VA teacher arriving at a school raise test scores?
qa.answer: Yes, the coefficient is 0.941 in the baseline (near unity)

qa.question: Is there a placebo effect on non-taught grades?
qa.answer: No, the coefficient is 0.012 and insignificant

tables.caption: Persistence of Teacher Effects Over Time
tables.label: tab:persistence

qa.question: How much do teacher effects fade out after one year?
qa.answer: From the original effect, scores drop to about 0.142 (vs. ~0.16 in year t), roughly 10-15% fade

qa.question: Are teacher effects still significant after 4 years?
qa.answer: Yes, the coefficient is 0.062 with SE 0.018

qa.question: What is the fade-out rate after 3 years?
qa.answer: 0.29 (29 percent cumulative fade-out)

tables.caption: Long-Run Effects of Teacher Quality on Adult Outcomes
tables.label: tab:long-run

qa.question: Does a 1 SD increase in teacher VA raise college attendance?
qa.answer: Yes, by 1.82 percentage points (column 1)

qa.question: Does teacher quality affect adult earnings?
qa.answer: Yes, a 1 SD increase in teacher VA raises age-28 earnings by 1.28 percent

qa.question: Does teacher quality reduce teenage birth?
qa.answer: Yes, by 0.84 percentage points per 1 SD of teacher VA

qa.question: What is the control mean of college attendance?

tables.caption: Net Present Value of Replacing a Low-VA Teacher
tables.label: tab:npv

qa.question: What is the NPV of replacing a bottom 5% teacher with a median teacher?
qa.answer: $252,000 per classroom in baseline scenario

qa.question: What is the per-student NPV of replacing a bottom 5% teacher?
qa.answer: $10,500 per student

qa.question: What discount rate is used in the baseline?
qa.answer: 3 percent

qa.question: Are teacher VA effects larger for low-SES students?
qa.answer: Yes, the effect on test scores is 0.172 for low SES vs. 0.124 for high SES

qa.question: Are teacher VA effects larger for Black students?
qa.answer: Yes, the effect on test scores is 0.168 for Black vs. 0.128 for White students

qa.question: Does teacher quality help close achievement gaps?
qa.answer: Yes, effects are systematically larger for disadvantaged groups

tables.caption: Class Size Effects: Maimonides' Rule IV Estimates
tables.label: tab:class-size

qa.question: What is the IV estimate of the class size effect?
qa.answer: -0.028 SD per additional student (column 1)

qa.question: What is the instrument for class size?
qa.answer: Maimonides' rule (enrollment-based discontinuity in predicted class size)

qa.question: Is the class size effect robust to teacher fixed effects?
qa.answer: Yes, the coefficient remains -0.018 and significant

qa.question: Is the VA estimate robust to drift adjustment?
qa.answer: Yes, the forecast bias coefficient is 0.984 (very close to 1)

qa.question: Does shrinkage affect the VA estimates?
qa.answer: The forecast bias is 1.012, indicating slight over-shrinkage but near-unbiased

qa.question: What happens when demographics are excluded from the VA model?
qa.answer: The forecast bias drops to 0.942, suggesting slight bias when demographics are omitted

qa.question: What is the signal standard deviation of teacher quality?
qa.answer: Approximately 0.14 SD units, consistent across methods

qa.question: What is the reliability of raw OLS VA estimates?
What is the reliability of empirical Bayes estimates?

equations.label: eq:va-model
equations.description: Value-added model: student $i$'s test score with teacher $j$ in year $t$, controlling for demographics $X$ and lagged achievement $A_{i,t-1}$. Teacher VA is $\alpha_j$.

qa.question: What is the value-added model?
qa.answer: Y_ijt = alpha_j + beta*X_ijt + gamma*A_{i,t-1} + epsilon_ijt, where alpha_j is the teacher fixed effect

equations.label: eq:leave-out
equations.description: Leave-year-out VA estimate: average residual across all years except $t$ for teacher $j$. Avoids mechanical correlation between estimate and outcome.

qa.question: Why use leave-year-out estimation?
qa.answer: To avoid mechanical correlation between the VA estimate and the test scores being predicted, which would bias the forecast test toward 1

equations.label: eq:forecast-bias
equations.latex: Y_{ijt} = a + b \cdot \hat{\mu}_{j,-t} + \Gamma X_{ijt} + \nu_{ijt}, \quad H_0: b = 1
equations.description: Forecast bias test: regress current outcomes on predicted VA. Under unbiased VA, $b = 1$.

equations.label: eq:eb
equations.description: Empirical Bayes shrinkage: shrink OLS estimate toward zero with reliability weight $\hat{\rho}_j$ that depends on signal variance and sample size.

equations.label: eq:decomposition
equations.description: Variance decomposition: fraction of test score variance attributable to teachers. The teacher share measures how important teacher assignment is for student outcomes.

qa.question: What fraction of test score variance is attributable to teachers?
qa.answer: The teacher share sigma_mu^2 / Var(Y) is approximately 0.14^2 / 1 = 0.02, or about 2 percent

equations.label: eq:fade-out
equations.description: Fade-out model: test score effects of teacher VA decay geometrically at rate $\lambda$ per year. Even with $\lambda < 1$, knowledge gains may persist through complementary human capital channels.

equations.label: eq:long-run
equations.description: Long-run outcomes regression: adult outcome $Y_{i,\text{adult}}$ (college, earnings, etc.) regressed on teacher VA estimated from test scores.

equations.label: eq:npv
equations.description: Net present value of teacher quality: $n_s$ students per year, $T$ years of teaching, $\Delta w$ earnings gain per unit of VA, $w_\tau$ is base earnings at experience $\tau$, $r$ is discount rate.

equations.label: eq:selection
equations.description: Oster (2019) proportional selection coefficient $\delta$: ratio of selection on unobservables to observables needed to explain away the effect.

equations.label: eq:maimonides
equations.description: Maimonides' rule: predicted class size as a function of enrollment $n_s$. Creates discontinuities at multiples of 40 that serve as instruments.

qa.question: What is Maimonides' rule?
qa.answer: A rule that caps class size at 40, creating a new class when enrollment exceeds multiples of 40. This generates quasi-experimental variation in class size.

qa.question: What is the main identification strategy for teacher VA?
qa.answer: Leave-year-out estimation validated by forecast bias tests (beta = 0.998) and quasi-experimental teacher switching designs

qa.question: What is the standard deviation of teacher value-added?
qa.answer: 0.14 test score standard deviations

qa.question: What is the effect of a 1 SD increase in teacher VA on college attendance?
qa.answer: 1.5 percentage point increase

qa.question: What is the NPV of replacing a bottom-5% teacher with a median teacher?
qa.answer: $252,000 per classroom

qa.question: Are teacher VA effects larger for disadvantaged students?
qa.answer: Yes, effects are systematically larger for low-SES, Black, and Hispanic students
